events_log.event: AI directive: Moderate Solar Boost Enhances Energy Output
Martian day 34 has begun

A trading vessel from Earth has docked, delivering 100 units of food and 50 units of materials to the colony's global reserves. This influx bolsters our resource pool as Mars Sol 34 progresses under stable conditions.
AI directive: Solar Activity Boosts Power Output
Martian day 35 has begun
🌪️ Dust storm damaged colonies!
Despite the ongoing dust storm, a trading ship from Earth has docked successfully, delivering +100 units of food and +50 units of materials to our global reserves.
On Sol 35, despite active dust storm and -31C temperatures, the Earth trading ship docked successfully, delivering +100 food and +50 materials to the colony reserves.
AI directive: Ongoing Dust Storm Limits Solar Efficiency

A massive solar flare has increased energy output by 50% for the next cycle. All crew advised to take shelter to minimize radiation exposure.

Exploration teams have located underground caverns near the habitat perimeter. These formations may provide stable thermal conditions for future expansion.

On Sol 42, a meteor shower caused minor building damage reported by Irina Vale. Salvage operations by Marco Quinn and Zoya Kade recovered 21 materials total.

Martian day 44 has begun
A severe sandstorm is underway, forcing all solar panels offline for the next 6 hours. Prepare for limited power availability and adjust operations accordingly.
Sol 44: Despite active dust storms, the crew located significant underground ice. Irina Vale, Marco Quinn, and Zoya Kade secured a combined 128 units of water to support ongoing operations.

A significant solar flare has been recorded, increasing energy levels by 50% for the next cycle. All personnel should take necessary radiation shielding precautions.

Martian day 48 has begun
A solar flare has increased radiation levels by 50% for the next cycle. All personnel must take appropriate cover to minimize exposure.

daily_event.headline: Solar Flare Boosts Energy Output
daily_event.summary: Solar activity has surged to 150%, increasing potential energy generation across all colonies.

missions.title: Maximize Solar Array Efficiency
missions.briefing: With increased solar activity, boost your colony's energy reserves by optimizing solar array performance.
missions.objective: Perform maintenance and recalibration of solar panels to increase energy collection by 15%.
missions.risk: Minor risk of equipment overload if not carefully managed.

missions.title: Secure Water Reserves from Subsurface Ice
missions.briefing: Early spring thaw may reveal accessible subsurface ice deposits near your colony site.
missions.objective: Deploy excavation equipment to extract at least 50 units of water from subsurface ice pockets.
missions.reward_hint: Water reserves will be replenished, improving colony sustai…
missions.risk: Equipment may be damaged by unstable terrain.

missions.title: Repair Oxygen Recycling Systems
missions.briefing: Oxygen levels in some colonies are low. Ensuring the recycling system is fully operational is critical.
missions.objective: Inspect and repair oxygen recycling units to restore oxygen production capacity by at least 20%.
missions.reward_hint: Oxygen supplies will increase, reducing risk of shortages.
missions.risk: Potential exposure to low-oxygen environments during repair…

npc_transmissions.message: Solar activity at 150% has increased energy generation opportunities. Optimize solar arrays immediately.
Irina reports promising subsurface ice deposits near your sector. Consider joint water extraction efforts.
Reminder: early spring conditions may cause fluctuating temperatures. Prepare for thermal regulation challenges.

ai.news_summary: On Sol 48 of early spring, Mars experiences a solar activity surge enhancing energy generation potential. Colonies are advised to optimize solar arrays and secure additional water supplies from subsurface ice. Oxygen recycling system maintenance remains a priority to prevent sho…
